messages.content: Build a TestScheduler-based unit test in RxJS v7 that verifies a debounceTime(30) pipeline using marble diagrams.
How do I use TestScheduler in RxJS v7 to test a map operator with marble syntax? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how TestScheduler virtual time works in RxJS v7, then show a working typed example testing delay(50).
Write an RxJS v7 TestScheduler test that verifies a switchMap pipeline cancels in-flight inner observables. Constraints: strict types, no any, use marble diagrams.
Build an RxJS v7 TestScheduler helper that tests a throttleTime(20) pipeline. Use object-form cold values and strict types.
Build a minimal example in RxJS v7 that uses asyncScheduler to schedule a delayed emission. Use of() and observeOn with a typed Observable<number>.
How do I pick between asyncScheduler, asapScheduler, and queueScheduler in RxJS v7? Show a minimal typed example that uses each.
Explain briefly how asapScheduler differs from queueScheduler in RxJS v7, then show a working typed example.
Write an RxJS v7 snippet that schedules an Observable emission via the asyncScheduler factory directly. Constraints: typed Observable<string>, no any.
Build an RxJS v7 throttled logger that uses queueScheduler to flush synchronously. Use Subject with a typed payload.
Refactor this RxJS v7 snippet so emissions use asapScheduler instead of default sync delivery: import { of } from 'rxjs'; of(1,2,3).subscribe(v => console.log(v));
Build a typed RxJS v7 pipeline that attaches wall-clock timestamps to each emission using the timestamp operator.
How do I measure gaps between emissions in RxJS v7? Show a minimal example using timeInterval.
Explain briefly how timestamp and timeInterval differ in RxJS v7, then show a working typed implementation using both.
Build an RxJS v7 latency monitor that logs the interval between heartbeat emissions. Use a typed Subject<void> and timeInterval.
Write an RxJS v7 pipeline that emits typed audit records. Constraints: each record must carry a timestamp produced by the timestamp operator, strict types, no any.
Add elapsed-time tagging to this RxJS v7 ticker so every emission includes the ms since the previous one: import { interval, take } from 'rxjs'; const t$ = interval(250).pipe(take(5));
Fix this v7 snippet so it attaches wall-clock timestamps using the timestamp operator rather than a manual Date.now(): of(1,2,3).pipe(map(v => ({ v, at: Date.now() })))
Build an RxJS v7 error-recovery pipeline that uses throwError and catchError to swap to a fallback Observable<User>.
How do I emit a lazy error in RxJS v7 without evaluating the Error eagerly at subscription time? Show a minimal example using throwError.
Explain briefly how catchError can rethrow a wrapped error in RxJS v7, then show a working typed implementation.
Write an RxJS v7 pipeline that swaps errors into a typed Result union via catchError. Constraints: strict types, no any, use throwError for a test failure.
Add a fallback branch to this RxJS v7 pipeline so errors recover into an empty list via catchError: const users$: Observable<User[]> = fetchUsers$();
How do I surface a typed error when a predicate fails in an RxJS v7 pipeline? Show a minimal example using throwError inside mergeMap.
Build an RxJS v7 guard that returns either a live$ or a cached$ Observable based on a boolean flag using iif.
How does iif decide which Observable to subscribe to in RxJS v7? Show a minimal typed example.
Explain briefly why iif is preferred over a ternary in RxJS v7, then show a working typed implementation.
Write an RxJS v7 reader that returns a live feed when authenticated and an empty feed otherwise. Constraints: use iif, strict types, no any.
Build an RxJS v7 cache-lookup router using iif where a hit returns the cached Observable and a miss fetches a fresh one.
Build an RxJS v7 pipeline that demonstrates concat, merge, and zip side by side. Use typed observables and label the output.
How do I pick between concat, merge, and zip in RxJS v7? Show a minimal typed example that highlights the difference.
Explain briefly when to use concat vs merge in RxJS v7, then show a working typed implementation that sequences two HTTP-like observables.
Write an RxJS v7 joiner that emits paired tuples from two independent sources. Constraints: use zip, strict types, no any.
Add a secondary event stream to this RxJS v7 ticker via merge so both emit into the same downstream: import { interval, take } from 'rxjs'; const ticks$ = interval(100).pipe(take(3));
Refactor this RxJS v7 snippet so it sequences the two observables with concat instead of firing them both in parallel via merge: const out$ = merge(phaseA$, phaseB$);
Build an RxJS v7 splitter that uses partition to fan a numeric stream into evens$ and odds$.
How does partition split a stream in RxJS v7? Show a minimal typed example.
Targeted-fix: update this pre-v7 partition call to the v7 standalone function form: import { partition } from 'rxjs/operators'; const split$ = source$.pipe(partition(p => p.ok));
Write an RxJS v7 router that splits user events into authenticated$ and anonymous$. Constraints: use partition, strict types, no any.
Build an RxJS v7 priority queue split: emit hot$ for priority >= 5 and cold$ otherwise, using partition and a typed Subject.
